summary: Iteration 2: Tested new WBS / Gantt / Workflow-drawer features + Create dialogs (Project, NCR, Hindrance, DPR). Backend: 13/13 new pytest tests pass (including WBS RBAC, cascade delete, level auto-calc, Gantt activities, Workflow history append). Frontend: CRITICAL build-error bug found and fixed — WbsTree.jsx triggered Babel 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' due to recursive <Node/> JSX pattern; rewrote with iterative flat-render. After fix, all new flows verified: Create Project dialog persists row, ProjectDetail Tabs (overview/wbs/gantt/packages/milestones) all present, WBS tree shows 17 default-expanded nodes (3 levels) and expand-toggle reveals children (+3), Gantt renders 42 bars across Engineering/Procurement/Construction with 35 wf-view buttons. Workflow drawer opens with priority/SLA/stage cards and history timeline; Closed (Approved) workflow correctly hides action buttons (matches spec).

ui_bugs.issue: Original recursive <Node/> JSX component caused Babel/babel-loader 'RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded' — entire frontend failed to compile. Fixed by testing agent: rewrote to iterative flat-render (useMemo + Set of expanded ids + while-loop flatten). Recommend main agent review/keep the new structure.

action_items: Review the rewritten /app/frontend/src/components/WbsTree.jsx (iterative version). Original recursive component is incompatible with current Babel/CRA toolchain — keep iterative approach or upgrade Babel.

(carry-over from iter1) Workflows page row-level Approve/Reject/Escalate buttons still render even for closed workflows — only the drawer correctly hides them. Consider gating row buttons by status too.
critical_code_review_comments: WbsTree.jsx recursive JSX with inner function reference triggered Babel infinite traversal — pattern to avoid in this CRA/Babel version.
server.py grew to ~1120 lines (still single-file). Consider splitting into routers (auth/projects/wbs/workflows/finance) when adding more features.
POST /api/wbs uses parent.level+1 but does NOT validate that the resulting level <= 10 even though product spec says 10-level tree — add bound check.

DELETE /api/wbs cascades only ONE level (children of root) — grandchildren of cascaded children are NOT deleted (db.wbs.delete_many({'id': c['id']}) just deletes the child itself, then grandchildren become orphaned). Should be recursive cascade.
/api/projects/{pid}/gantt has no project-existence check — returns [] for invalid pid instead of 404.
PATCH /api/wbs allows SiteEngineer to update including planned_start/end and weightage — may want to restrict to progress/actual_start/actual_end for SiteEngineer.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/WbsTree.jsx (rewritten iterative to fix Babel build failure)
test_credentials: admin@mecon.in / Mecon@2026 (admin) and contractor@lnt.com / Demo@2026 (RBAC) — both verified.
seed_data_creation: No new seed. WBS auto-seeded on startup (~59 items / project across L1-L4). Activities ~42/project. Created TEST_WBS_ROOT (cleaned up via cascade-delete test) and TESTUI-B1 project via UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: WbsTree was originally written with a recursive React function `Node({...})` that called itself via JSX `<Node .../>` inside `.map()`. The CRA Babel toolchain in this project chokes on that pattern with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' even after renaming. The iterative version (flatten via while-loop with expanded Set) compiles cleanly. Keep this pattern. Backend new tests are in test_wbs_gantt_workflow.py. Existing test_mecon_backend.py still applies.
rca of the issue: WbsTree.jsx: Babel/babel-loader threw RangeError during AST traversal of the recursive React component. The recursive JSX `function Node({...}) { ... <Node .../> ... }` pattern combined with template-literal styles and arbitrary-Tailwind class strings inside the same JSX expression caused babel-traverse to enter an infinite loop in this CRA's Babel version. Rename (Node → WbsNodeRow) alone did not help; the only reliable fix was to make the rendering iterative (flatten the tree once via while-loop, then render a single flat list). Reproduce by restoring the recursive version — webpack will fail compile with 'Maximum call stack size exceeded'. Fix: iterative flat-render in /app/frontend/src/components/WbsTree.jsx (now committed).
